top_stories.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in the Microsoft Entra ID "Agent ID Administrator" role that could enable privilege escalation and service principal takeover attacks. Silverfort researchers discovered that this built-in role, designed to manage AI agent identity lifecycle operations, could be exploited to gain unauthorized control over identities within Entra ID.

top_stories.title: Microsoft fixes Entra ID flaw enabling privilege escalation
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a privilege escalation vulnerability in Microsoft Entra ID where the Agent ID Administrator role could be abused to enable account takeover of service accounts. The flaw allowed attackers with that role to escalate privileges beyond their intended permissions within the Entra ID identity platform.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra Agent ID Flaw Enabled Tenant Takeover via Privilege Escalation
top_stories.summary: A flaw in Microsoft Entra Agent ID allowed attackers to escalate privileges and take over entire tenants through Service Principal abuse. Microsoft has fully patched the vulnerability.

top_stories.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
top_stories.summary: A security analysis examines the Vercel breach, focusing on how a compromised third-party OAuth integration created a direct attack path into customer environments. The incident illustrates risks from OAuth sprawl and shadow AI integrations, where unauthorized or poorly governed OAuth app connections can lead to widespread downstream customer impact.

top_stories.title: cPanel zero-day exploited for months before patch release (CVE-2026-41940)
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) in cPanel has been actively exploited in the wild since at least February 23, months before a patch was released. The flaw affects cPanel, a widely used web hosting control panel, and was analyzed by watchTowr security researchers.

news.title: Identity is the control plane for distributed infrastructure
news.summary: Teleport CEO Ev Kontsevoy argues that distributed infrastructure across cloud, Kubernetes, databases, and servers requires a unified identity layer rather than additional tools layered on fragmented identity systems. He advocates for fewer credentials, fewer entry points, and a single identity plane providing unified visibility and control for security and engineering teams.

news.title: Critical cPanel and WHM bug exploited as a zero-day, PoC now available
news.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) in cPanel, WHM, and WP Squared is being actively exploited as a zero-day. The flaw allows attackers to bypass authentication controls, with exploitation attempts observed since late February and a proof-of-concept now publicly available.

news.title: Nine-year-old Linux kernel flaw enables reliable local privilege escalation (CVE-2026-31431)
news.summary: Security researchers at Theori disclosed a high-severity local privilege escalation vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431) in the Linux kernel, present in virtually all major Linux distributions since 2017. A working proof-of-concept exploit is publicly available for the flaw, which stems from interactions between multiple kernel changes made over several years.

news.title: Official SAP npm packages compromised to steal credentials
news.summary: Multiple official SAP npm packages were compromised in a supply-chain attack attributed to TeamPCP, targeting developers' systems. The attack was designed to steal credentials and authentication tokens from affected systems.

news.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted around the threat of AI-automated attacks targeting Active Directory and Domain Admin credentials. The session focuses on autonomous agents capable of mapping Active Directory and escalating privileges within minutes, framed around exposure validation workflows.

news.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
news.summary: cPanel has released security updates to address a critical authentication vulnerability affecting all currently supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM). The flaw impacts various authentication paths and could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to the control panel software.

news.title: Organizing Agents’ memory at scale: Namespace design patterns in AgentCore Memory
news.summary: AWS published a technical post covering namespace design patterns for AgentCore Memory, including the use of IAM-based access control. The IAM component is one element of a broader agentic AI architecture discussion rather than a dedicated IAM topic.

news.title: AWS Transfer Family Terraform module now supports Okta and Microsoft Entra ID integration examples
news.summary: AWS has updated its Transfer Family Terraform module to include end-to-end deployment examples integrating Okta and Microsoft Entra ID as custom identity providers for authentication and access control. The update enables enterprises using these IdPs to authenticate and manage access to AWS Transfer Family endpoints using their existing identity infrastructure.

news.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
news.summary: A research report called "Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace," based on a survey of 500 security professionals, examines how secure data movement creates bottlenecks in Zero Trust program implementation. The article argues that connectivity assumptions undermine Zero Trust deployments, though the focus is primarily on data movement rather than core IAM controls.

news.title: Access control with IAM Identity Center session tags
news.summary: AWS published guidance on using IAM Identity Center session tags for access control across multi-account environments. The article covers how organizations can leverage session tags within IAM Identity Center to manage workforce access in a scalable and centralized manner.

news.title: Why AI agents are triggering a rethink of enterprise identity
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how agentic AI is affecting enterprise identity and access management practices. The article focuses on the specific security stack impacts of AI agents, which require a rethink of traditional IAM approaches.

news.title: Silverfort acquires Fabrix Security to bring AI decision-making to runtime access control
news.summary: Silverfort has acquired Fabrix Security, an AI-native identity security startup founded in 2024, for an undisclosed sum. The acquisition is aimed at integrating AI-driven decision-making capabilities into runtime access control within Silverfort's unified identity security platform.

news.title: Simplifying AWS defense with Microsoft Sentinel UEBA
news.summary: Microsoft Sentinel's User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) capability is being applied to AWS CloudTrail logs to help distinguish legitimate user activity from attacker behavior. The approach enriches raw log data with behavioral signals based on baseline patterns from users, peers, and devices to improve detection in cloud environments.

news.title: Five-minute interview: Zair Abbas, Okta
news.summary: Okta's partner ecosystem lead for EMEA, Zair Abbas, participated in a brief personal interview format. The article is a short profile piece sharing personal insights from a channel executive rather than substantive IAM news.

news.title: How to fix cybersecurity's agentic AI identity crisis
news.summary: The article addresses security challenges posed by autonomous AI agents in enterprise environments, focusing on identity and access management for non-human digital actors. The piece covers how agentic AI systems require new approaches to authentication, authorization, and access control frameworks.

news.title: Can I do that with policy? Understanding the AWS Service Authorization Reference
news.summary: AWS published guidance explaining the capabilities and limitations of AWS IAM policies, covering service control policies, permission boundaries, and resource-based policies. The article addresses how AWS Identity and Access Management policy types interact to help practitioners build effective security controls within AWS environments.

news.title: Why AI is forcing a reset of the identity stack
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how AI is driving changes to identity and access management approaches, framing a conceptual shift from identity management to identity intelligence. The article is part of a series exploring the intersection of AI and IAM.

news.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
news.summary: Microsoft will begin rolling out passkey support for Microsoft Entra-protected resources on Windows devices starting late April. The feature enables phishing-resistant passwordless authentication for Entra-managed environments.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign required no malware deployment, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
